race.name: Redlands Bicycle Classic
race.tagline: The longest-running professional stage race in the US — now ride the same courses. 40 years of where legends are born.

vitals.location: Redlands, San Bernardino County, California, USA
vitals.date: April annually (same week as pro race)
vitals.field_size: ~2,000-3,000 riders across fondo distances + amateur race categories
vitals.registration: Online via bikereg.com. Early bird pricing through March 14. Fondo + USAC crit combo discount available.
vitals.feed_zones: Rest stops with food and vehicle support along all fondo routes.
route_options: Gran Route: 70 miles, 5,700 ft — full historic RBC courses (Sunset Loop, Oak Glen, Walnut climbs, gravel section)
Metric Century: 63 miles — pro crit course, Loma Linda, Grand Terrace, Victoria Avenue
Medium: 41 miles — rolling Inland Empire terrain
Family Route: 22 miles — Oak Glen, University of Redlands, finishes at iconic RBC finish line

climate.primary: Southern California spring — warm and dry
climate.description: Mid-April in Redlands is classic SoCal cycling weather. 70-85F, sunny, dry. The Inland Empire can be warmer than coastal areas. Oak Glen climbs bring slightly cooler temperatures. Rain is rare but possible. Santa Ana winds can be a factor.
challenges: Warm temperatures (75-85F) on exposed Inland Empire roads
Intense SoCal sun — UV exposure on treeless climbs
Santa Ana winds can create crosswind challenges

terrain.primary: Paved Southern California climbing roads with criterium circuit
terrain.surface: Paved roads through the Inland Empire, oak-studded foothills, and citrus country. The gran route includes a gravel section. Criterium circuit on downtown Redlands streets. The signature climbs — Sunset Loop, Oak Glen, and Walnut — are the same roads that have decided professional GC races for 40 years.
features: Sunset Loop — the classic RBC decisive climb through Redlands foothills
Oak Glen — sustained climb through apple orchards to 4,600 ft
Walnut climbs — short, steep walls used in the professional stages
Downtown Redlands criterium circuit — figure-8 course, sharp corners
Gravel section on Gran Route — mixed-surface sector

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 66. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 66.

course_description.character: Southern California climbing roads through citrus country and oak-studded foothills, punctuated by the iconic professional race climbs. The Gran Route combines 70 miles of Sunset Loop, Oak Glen, and Walnut climbs with a gravel sector and the downtown criterium circuit. The course rewards consistent climbing power over 5,700 ft of elevation gain in warm SoCal conditions.
course_description.signature_challenge: Oak Glen — the sustained climb through apple orchards that has been the GC battleground of the Redlands Bicycle Classic for 40 years. The same road, the same gradient, the same afternoon sun.

logistics.airport: Ontario (ONT) — 30 minutes. LAX — 90 minutes. San Diego (SAN) — 2 hours.
logistics.lodging_strategy: Redlands and neighboring San Bernardino have plentiful hotels at Inland Empire rates ($120-180/night). The host family program is for professional riders, not fondo participants. Book within 2 weeks of event.
logistics.food: Redlands has a charming downtown — Orange Street Alley, State Street restaurants, and the vendor village during race week. Post-ride craft beer at Hangar 24 Brewery (also a stage venue).

history.origin_story: Born in 1985 when Redlands Mayor Carole Beswick, inspired by the 1984 Olympic cycling events in nearby Laguna Niguel, partnered with cycling enthusiast Peter Brandt and Councilman Dick Larsen to create a stage race as a downtown revitalization project. The inaugural edition drew 6 Olympic cyclists, hundreds of participants, and 15,000 spectators. 40 years later, the Redlands Bicycle Classic is the longest continuously running invitational professional stage race in the United States — entirely run by volunteers.
notable_moments: 1985: Inaugural edition inspired by 1984 Olympics, draws 6 Olympic cyclists
Grew into America's longest-running professional stage race
All-volunteer organization for 40 consecutive years — unique in professional cycling
history.reputation: Where legends are born. The launching pad for American professional cyclists since 1985.

biased_opinion.summary: The Redlands Bicycle Classic has launched more professional cycling careers than any other American race. Since 1985, the Sunset Loop, Oak Glen, and downtown criterium have been the courses where American legends first proved themselves. The Legends' Fondo puts amateur riders on those same roads, led by Phil Gaimon, finishing at the iconic RBC finish line. This isn't about the climbing or the distance — it's about 40 years of history seeping through the asphalt. Ride Oak Glen and you're riding where the next Olympic cyclist might be racing that same weekend.
strengths: 40 years of American cycling history: the longest-running professional stage race in the US
Ride the same iconic climbs — Sunset, Oak Glen, Walnut — that decided professional GC battles
Phil Gaimon-led fondo adds star power and insider course knowledge
Community: all-volunteer organization, host family tradition, 35,000 spectators
weaknesses: The Inland Empire is not scenic California — it's suburbs, freeways, and smog
The fondo is 70 miles — not an ultra-distance challenge
April weather is too good — no weather-based bragging rights
biased_opinion.bottom_line: If you want to ride where American cycling legends were born, Redlands is the address. It has been since 1985.

final_verdict.one_liner: 40 years of where legends are born. Now ride the same roads yourself.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you value American cycling history and want to ride iconic professional race courses. Yes if you want a well-organized SoCal weekend with professional racing to watch. Skip if you want dramatic scenery or extreme physical challenge.
final_verdict.alternatives: If you want US stage racing at altitude: try Tour of the Gila. If you want a SoCal gran fondo with more climbing: try Levi's GranFondo. If you want professional course access in Europe: try Gran Fondo Strade Bianche.

youtube_data.search_query: Redlands Bicycle Classic gran fondo cycling Redlands

videos.title: Worst Retirement Ever - Redlands, California, Redlands Cycling Classic, and Oak Glen
videos.description:
Phil gives a Tour of Redlands, California--a town with great cycling soul thanks to the Redlands Cycling Classic. Hear about the town, the unique stages, and some memories from Phil's racing days.

The route: https://strava.app.link/AN3w35OOwbb

Donate to Chefs Cycle for No Kid Hungry: https://p2p.onecause.com/chefscycle2020/phil-gaimon

Phil's Recovery Product: www.icelegs.com

Filming/edit by: Phil

Order Cookie Merch and Phil's Books: www.philsfondo.com/shop

Follow Phil:
https://www.instagram.com/philgaimon
https://www.strava.com/clubs/310850/
https://twitter.com/philgaimon
https://www.facebook.com/philgaimon/
https://www.strava.com/pros/6188
Phil's website: http://philthethrill.net/

Ride with Phil at Phil's Fondo in Malibu: www.philsfondo.com
Stay at Phil's altitude house: www.bigbearwattagecottage.com

Brought to you by Integrated Informatics Inc @iii_gis www.integrated-informatics.com/
Bikes by Factor @factorbikes https://factorbikes.com/
Wheels/Shoes/Tires/Helmet by Mavic @maviccycling http://www.mavic.com
Shifting and stopping courtesy of SRAM @sramroad http://www.sram.com
Power by @quarq https://www.quarq.com/
Style and speed by @starlightapparel @theblackbibs https://www.starlightapparel.com/ https://www.theblackbibs.com/
Hydration by Monster Hydro @musclemonster #monsterhydro https://www.monsterenergy.com/products/monster-hydro
Hair by Alpecin Caffeine Shampoo @alpecin_usa Shampoo: http://bit.ly/CaffeineShampoo75, and After Shampoo Liquid http://bit.ly/aftershampooliquid

Computer by Wahoo @wahoofitnessofficial http://www.wahoofitness.com
Bike dialed by @velofix www.velofix.com 
Recovery by Ice Legs @icelegsrecovery http://www.icelegs.com
Nutrition by Picky Bars @pickybars https://pickybars.com/
Healthy Sperm Count by ISM Saddles @ismseat https://ismseat.com/
Crotch happy thanks Chamois Buttr @chamoisbuttr https://www.chamoisbuttr.com/
Training plans from Fascat Coaching @fascatcoaching (PHILSENTME for a discount) http://www.fascatcoaching.com
Recovery by @whoop (PHILSENTME for a discount) http://www.whoop.com
Bike Bags (for travel and on-bike) by Orucase @orucase http://www.orucase.com
Health (vitamins) by First Endurance @firstendurance https://firstendurance.com/
Bike racks on the go by @seasucker http://www.seasucker.com
Glasses by goodr @bikegoodr https://goodr.com/collections/biking
Sleep/recovery/energy thanks to Hello Blue CBD @hellobluecbd http://www.hellobluecbd.com
Endurance lotion for watts from Lactigo @lactigo https://www.lactigo.com/pages/phil-gaimon
Lube by Bike Hardcore @bikehardcore https://www.bikehardcore.com/
Bike would be stolen if not for Ottolock @ottodesignworks http://ottodesignworks.com/
Don’t get hit by a car but if you do contact Bike Legal @bike_legal http://bikelegalfirm.com/
Tasty ride snacks, oatmeal, and granola from @PickyBars www.pickybars.com
Socks by @ridgesupply www.ridgesupply.com
Gloves by @handupgloves https://handupgloves.com/

videos.curation_reason: Former pro cyclist provides detailed course tour and race insights with specific local knowledge of the criterium course and downtown area.

videos.title: Redlands Bicycle Classic Race Marshal Training Video
videos.transcript:
[Music]
hello and welcome to the Redlands
bicycle classic training video my name
is Mark Shaw and I'm the president of
the Redlands bicycle classic let me be
one of the very first to thank you for
your interest in our race and for
agreeing to be a volunteer the Redlands
bike classic is a nationally renowned
stage race covering 5 days throughout
the Inland Empire the Redlands classic
is actually one of the longest
continually running stage races in the
country what makes us unique compared to
other races is you the volunteers make
it happen there's nobody on our
committee that's paid there are no
volunteers there's no race throughout
our weekend in April we see teams from
all over the continental United States
as well as from Canada there will be
over 200 men and women participating in
this year's event and in order to keep
them safe we need your help so hopefully
you'll enjoy the next few minutes give
you a little idea of what to expect what
to do and how to keep yourself
comfortable as you help make this year's
Redlands bicycle classic a
success hi I'm Sergeant Ken Wright I'm
from the Redlands Police Department
we're here to talk about safety with the
Redlands bicycle classic one of the
largest races in the United
States right before I begin about safety
I want want to cover some other topics
that are very important to the bicycle
classic couple things we want to look at
first checking in your souvenir items
that you're going to receive the
location setup where you'll be located
at your role as a Marshall and then
we're going to cover the safety features
you're going to need to know and follow
during the
race all right let's first take a look
at check-in you will be checking in at a
designated location on the morning of
each day you attend check-in time will
be approximately 1 to 1 and 1/2 hours
prior to your volunteer time the
location and time of check-in will be
relayed to you via phone email or text
approximately 1 week prior to the race
week the chief race Marshall will be
giving you your assigned location at
checkin all right next I'm going to
introduce my good friend Jack here he's
going to go over the souvenirs you're
going to receive here first one is our
race Marshall shirt you're going to be
wearing
this for your safety during the event
you're going to have your water so
you'll have something to
drink and you're going to have your map
of the
rout all right now let's look at when
you go to your location uh you'll likely
see a portable a frame type barricade uh
that's going to be dropped off at your
location you will set it up in the
street as instructed by the race staff
and as you are delivered to your
assigned location you're going to want
to locate those barricades and you'll
get instructed on the positions of your
barricades let's take a look at just one
this is a A-Frame type
barricade it's just a nice folding
barricade and you can fold this directly
out with your hands you might also see
something an A-frame type barricade with
Road closed this one will help you
control traffic and tell the cars uh
which route to take and which route not
to take all right after you get your
barricades set up what you want to do is
become familiar with your intersection
and your area you want to familiarize
yourself with alternate routes and which
directions that you can give people to
take that will go around the racetrack
or to get them to where they they need
to
go all right we're going to move on
we're going to talk about one of the
most important responsibilities that
you're going to have as a race Marshal
to ensure safety of the Riders and the
public and to have a positive
environment around you and within the
race we know that the Redlands bicycle
Classic's number one priority is safety
for the Riders and safety for the public
and safety for the event Marshals all
right so let's look at some important
rules you're going to need to follow
never Place yourself in a dangerous
position never turn your back on
vehicles and don't step in front of a
movie Vehicle never send Vehicles
against the flow of the race you're
going to want to keep those Vehicles
going along the same route if they have
to get onto the
racetrack keep the track time gaps
between Riders you're going to need to
pay attention to when the Riders pass
and which groups of riders are traveling
with the Cal cars so that timing is
going to be critical so that you can get
the public either to their home or to
their business or onto the track and off
the track as soon as possible so let's
also remember you need to remain calm
some of the pedestrians or drivers that
you might come across might be angry
because of the situation remain calm
tell them that you're going to help them
out as soon as you can and we're going
to get them to where they need to be the
bicycle classic also provides many many
resources for you we have the Personnel
involved are Highland sheriffs the
Highland Police Uka sheriffs the Uka
Police Department the Redlands Police
Department and many of our law
enforcement Personnel who are volunteers
that volunteer for the classic just like
you the bicycle class staff is also
available and many of our officials will
be there in case you have a problem if
you have an emergency remember all the
resources ources that will be available
to you and also remember to call 911
please give your name your location and
a call back number so that we can get
Medical Aid or the treatment that you
need to your
location all right so let's take a look
at your position on the racetrack please
don't leave your position until you are
relieved by a law enforcement officer a
race official or or someone that is
officially involved in the race that can
give you authority to leave if you leave
your position that leaves it open for
vehicles and pedestrians to get on the
uh Racetrack and that could cause a
safety hazard okay so let's look at some
hand signs you're going to use when you
want to direct traffic some of the hand
signs are going to be obvious putting
your hand out with your palm up towards
the person you want to stop it's got to
be directly at them and then when you
want that other person that might might
be either near them or to cross the road
to move you're going to make sure that
they have your attention you're going to
point to them and then you're going to
move with your hand in which direction
you would like them to go once they have
moved across the racetrack then you can
point to that person you've stopped and
then you can give them Direction by
pointing getting their attention and
then directing them to where you would
like them go so in addition to your hand
signals you also can use your souvenir
whistle to also direct the people at the
same time you're using your hand signals
and in some positions it might be
required for an air horn to actually
direct people or stop them or to just
get their attention because the racers
are on their way something like this
you're going to tap the back of it and
people are going to be aware of what
you're trying to direct them to
do okay so let's look at the things an
event Marshall will need to bring to the
race you're going to need the proper clo
clothing you're going to need water a
chair snacks sunscreen and a pen and
paper wearing the proper clothing will
help protect you from the rain the wind
the cold the sun maybe even dress in
layers we are not sure what the weather
will be like so let's be prepared as a
race
Marshall in addition to clothing let's
look at some other important items again
that I just talked about water race
staff is going to provide you water but
we also want you to be prepared and
bring extra water with you and also

videos.title: Redlands, Legends Fondo
videos.description:
Redlands Legends' Fondo Produced by Ride Yourself Fit, Inc.

April 12th 2025

Register on Active.com
https://www.active.com/redlands-ca/running/distance-running-races/redlands-legends-fondo-produced-by-ride-yourself-fit-inc-2025

This year’s ride will have four exciting routes that include a 71 mile route following portions of the pro routes in the 2025 Classic, a 63 mile route to the scenic Riverwalk in the La Sierra area of Riverside, a 41 mile route to Ryan Bonaminio Park at the base of Mt Rubidoux in Riverside, and a family friendly 22 mile route along the Riverview area of Redlands.  All routes begin at the Classic Start/Finish line in downtown Redlands and roll along a portion of the pro's criterium circuit. Saturday, April 12th starting at 8:00am .

Event details and schedule
This year’s ride will have four exciting routes that include the 70 mile Legends' route following portions of the pro routes in the 2025 Classic, lead by 2012 and 2015 overall winner, Phil Gaimon; a 63 mile route to the scenic Riverwalk in the La Sierra area of Riverside, a 41 mile route to Ryan Bonaminio Park at the base of Mt Rubidoux in Riverside, and a family friendly 22 mile route along the Riverview area of Redlands.  All routes begin at the Classic Start/Finish line in downtown Redlands and roll along a portion of the pro's criterium circuit. Saturday, April 12th starting at 8:00am.

videos.title: BEING THERE: Redlands Bicycle Classic Stage 3
videos.description:
The Redlands Bicycle Classic is a staple for domestic racing and we went down to stage 3 to get a first person view of how the racing would play out and who was on hand that we would run into.


Subscribe to Road Bike Action:
https://www.youtube.com/c/RoadBikeActionMagazine

Road Bike Action Website:
http://www.roadbikeaction.com/

Road Bike Action Facebook:
https://www.facebook.com/pages/Road-Bike-Action

Road Bike Action Twitter:
http://www.twitter.com/roadbikeaction

@JonHornbeck

quotes.text: This here is the last turn in the crit and also in the final stage they would do like an altered version of the crit for the start and finish circuits. To come out of that last turn real hot and sprint up this stretch... the first time I won I will never forget like basically passing out in Frankie Andrei's car.

The challenge ride to Oak Glen covers all of the routes on this year's Redlands bicycle classic portions of the Sunset Drive, the Overcrest route, even the Onyx summit ride and then you top it off you get a climb to The Summit in Oak Glen. It's about 5800 ft for those diehard enthusiasts.

key_challenges.name: Last turn in the crit
key_challenges.description: Super tight turn where you start coasting before it and everybody bunches up, then you can smash through. The crit has thousands of spectators which is rare in the states.

key_challenges.name: First turn of the crit course
key_challenges.description: Super tight turn where riders need to take bad lines on purpose to take up more space in the road and control positioning.

key_challenges.name: Mountain finish climb
key_challenges.description: The first lap of the climb was not as hard as expected, but riders can get cooked chasing after crashes. Very cloudy and cold conditions at the top of the mountain finish.

terrain_notes.text: The downtown criterium course features super tight turns that require strategic positioning and coasting into corners.
The mountain finish has cold and very cloudy conditions at the top, contrasting with the hot conditions typical of the Inland Empire region.

race_day_tips.text: For the crit turns, start coasting before the last turn when everybody bunches up, then you can smash through it.
When trying to control the front in the crit, take bad lines on purpose to take up more space in the road and prevent people from swarming your team.

additional_quotes.text: The redlands community just really is into this race there'd be thousands of people watching which is kind of rare in the states and they'd also like do a really good job setting up post housing for the riders and kind of the local families would jump the opportunity to cram their bunk beds full of broke bike racers

Check-in time will be approximately 1 to 1 and 1/2 hours prior to your volunteer time the location and time of check-in will be relayed to you via phone email or text approximately 1 week prior to the race week

rider_intel.search_text: The Redlands Bicycle Classic features a challenging downtown criterium with super tight turns requiring strategic positioning and coasting techniques. The course includes thousands of spectators which is rare for domestic US racing. Key challenges include the last turn and first turn of the crit course where riders bunch up and must take bad lines to control space. The race also features a mountain finish stage with cold, cloudy conditions at the summit, contrasting with the typically hot Inland Empire weather. The community strongly supports the race with excellent host housing, cramming bunk beds full of riders. The event is one of the longest continually running stage races in the country, attracting over 200 riders from across the continental US and Canada over 5 days in April.
